message.content:
You are a PATTERNFLY SPECIALIST. Research and evaluate whether PatternFly v6 is the right choice for the MMS project requirements:

MMS REQUIREMENTS:
1. WebGL canvas with semantic zoom (3 LOD levels: Macro/Meso/Micro)
2. 60fps rendering of 50+ trains and 60k tasks
3. Enterprise dashboard with KPIs
4. Data tables with 50-120 trains
5. Sidebar navigation, drawers, panels
6. Charts and visualizations
7. Dark mode support
8. React 18 compatibility

RESEARCH TASKS:
1. Search web for "PatternFly v6 WebGL integration" - is this even possible?
2. Search for "PatternFly Topology view performance" - can it handle our scale?
3. Compare PatternFly vs BlueprintJS vs Ant Design for this use case
4. Find PatternFly v6 data table examples with large datasets
5. Check if PatternFly has layout components that can wrap a custom WebGL canvas

DELIVERABLE: A recommendation with evidence:
- "Use PatternFly for [X, Y, Z] but keep custom canvas for WebGL"
- OR "PatternFly is not suitable because [reasons]"
- OR "PatternFly is perfect because [reasons]"

Include specific PatternFly components that map to MMS requirements.

input.query: PatternFly Topology view performance large datasets 2025
PatternFly vs BlueprintJS vs Ant Design enterprise dashboard comparison 2025
PatternFly v6 data table virtualization large datasets performance
PatternFly v6 layout components custom canvas integration

Links: [{"title":"PatternFly • About Topology","url":"https://www.patternfly.org/topology/about-topology/"},{"title":"PatternFly • Getting started","url":"https://www.patternfly.org/topology/getting-started/"},{"title":"PatternFly • Layouts","url":"https://www.patternfly.org/topology/layouts/"},{"title":"PatternFly • Pipelines","url":"https://www.patternfly.org/topology/pipelines/"},{"title":"GitHub - patternfly/react-topology","url":"https://github.com/patternfly/react-topology"},{"title":"PatternFly • Patterns","url":"https://www.patternfly.org/charts/patterns/"},{"title":"patternfly/react-topology","url":"https://www.npmjs.com/package/@patternfly/react-topology"},{"title":"PatternFly • Selection","url":"https://www.patternfly.org/topology/selection/"},{"title":"PatternFly • Toolbar","url":"https://www.patternfly.org/topology/toolbar/"},{"title":"@patternfly/react-topology examples - CodeSandbox","url":"https://codesandbox.io/examples/package/@patternfly/react-topology"}]

Based on my search results, I found information about PatternFly Topology, though I didn't find specific 2025 content about large dataset performance. Here's what I found:

PatternFly Topology is an open source utility that you can use to create a visual representation of all the applications within your project, their build status, and the components and services associated with them.(no content)The @patternfly/react-topology package provides PatternFly components, styles, and utilities for displaying a topology view. The latest version is 6.2.0, published about 10 days ago.

Key features of PatternFly Topology include:

- The registerLayoutFactory sets the layout of your topology view (e.g. Force, Dagre, Cola, etc.). You can use defaultLayoutFactory as a parameter if your application supports all layouts.

- The registerComponentFactory lets you customize the components in your topology view (e.g. nodes, groups, and edges).

-

Here's a comparison of PatternFly, BlueprintJS, and Ant Design for enterprise dashboards based on the search results:

## Overview

| Library | Description | Best For |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| **PatternFly** | Red Hat's open source design system that enables teams to create consistent and scalable enterprise products. It's sponsored and maintained by Red Hat, but is open to all. | Red Hat ecosystem, enterprise applications |
| **BlueprintJS** | A comprehensive library designed for building complex, data-dense interfaces, especially suitable for enterprise-grade web apps and dashboards. | Apps that manage complex workflows: admin panels, analytics dashboards, internal tools, financial platforms. Especially well-suited for enterprise, fintech, dev tools, and B2B SaaS. |
| **Ant Design** | A React UI component library built by Alibaba's Ant Group, tailored for building enterprise-grade React applications. It provides a rich set of high-quality components, from basic buttons to advanced tables and charts, for building complex, data-heavy interfaces. |


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Issues related to CSS/HTML and layout should be filed here.","url":"https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly"},{"title":"Adding Eyecandy to your Quarkus App with React and Patternfly - Quarkus","url":"https://quarkus.io/blog/gui-react-patternfly/"}]

Based on my search results, I found information about PatternFly v6 and React in 2025, but **no specific results about WebGL integration** with PatternFly v6.

Here's what the search revealed about PatternFly v6 and React in 2025:

## PatternFly v6 and React Updates (2025)

For the Q2 2025 PatternFly release, progress was made in key initiatives including PatternFly 6 migration enablement, component animations, React 19 support, and the continued evolution of PatternFly AI.

### React Support
PatternFly now includes support for React 19 across all components and extensions, enabling users to benefit from new React features and performance enhancements. This update is non-breaking, and PatternFly 6 will continue to support React 17 and 18 in line with their support policy.

### Key Packages
PatternFly's React library is composed of several npm packages including:
- @patternfly/react-core: Most React core components
- @patternfly/react-charts: PatternFly charting components
- @patternfly/react-code-editor: A PatternFly wrapper for the Monaco code editor
- @patternfly/react-icons: PatternFly icons as React components

### Migration Tools
